comments.body: still not work. inprogress files are still produced, the checkpoint folder is created but no chk-0,1,2... folder appeared, and the temp data file is not rolled.

Make Sure the TableEnvironment is using the same StreamExecutionEnvironment. If you're creating a StreamExecutionEnvironment with checkpointing, but not passing it to the StreamTableEnvironment, checkpoints won’t apply to your job. EnvironmentSettings settings = EnvironmentSettings.newInstance() .inStreamingMode() .build(); StreamTableEnvironment tableEnv = StreamTableEnvironment.create(env, settings); Make sure env here is the one with checkpointing enabled.
I think it is not the point cuz when I sink as json, everything is still smooth :<
I think the problem is it cannot sink to parquet bc the temp file size is always 0KB :))

answers.body:
You're writing to Parquet, which is a bulk format. As such, files are only rolled on checkpoints, so you have to use CheckpointRollingPolicy. See bulk encoded formats, where it says

Important Bulk Formats can only have a rolling policy that extends the CheckpointRollingPolicy. The latter rolls on every checkpoint. A policy can roll additionally based on size or processing time.

title: Files stuck as .inprogress, not rolling into final Parquet files
body:
I'm running a Flink streaming job using the Table API, which reads from Kafka and writes to S3 (for now I'm using a local path to simulate S3). The job uses a filesystem connector to write data in Parquet format.

Here is a simplified version of my code:

tableEnv.executeSql(
    "CREATE TABLE logs_source (" +
    " `timestamp` STRING," +
    " user_id INT," +
    " message STRING" +
    ") WITH (" +
    " 'connector' = 'kafka'," +
    " 'topic' = 'kafka-5s'," +
    " 'properties.bootstrap.servers' = 'localhost:9092'," +
    " 'properties.group.id' = 'kafka-5s-1'," +
    " 'scan.startup.mode' = 'earliest-offset'," +
    " 'format' = 'json'," +
    " 'json.timestamp-format.standard' = 'ISO-8601'" +
    ")"
);

tableEnv.executeSql(
    "CREATE TABLE s3_sink (" +
    " `timestamp` STRING," +
    " user_id INT," +
    " message STRING" +
    ") WITH (" +
    " 'connector' = 'filesystem'," +
    " 'path' = 'file:///D://flink-data/data'," +
    " 'format' = 'parquet'," +
    " 'parquet.compression' = 'SNAPPY'," +
    " 'sink.rolling-policy.rollover-interval' = '1000'," +
    " 'sink.rolling-policy.file-size' = '10'," +
    " 'sink.rolling-policy.inactivity-interval' = '1000'" +
    ")"
);

tableEnv.executeSql("INSERT INTO s3_sink SELECT `timestamp`, user_id, message FROM logs_source");

Problem:

Flink does write .inprogress files in the target directory (e.g., part-0-0.inprogress), but these files never finalize into proper .parquet files.

What I’ve tried:

  • Setting short rollover-interval, file-size, and inactivity-interval values (1000 ms and 10 bytes) to trigger quick roll.

  • Enabled checkpointing with:

        Configuration config = GlobalConfiguration.loadConfiguration(
                "D:\\Gitlab\\java-project\\src\\main\\resources\\"
        );

        config.set(CheckpointingOptions.CHECKPOINT_STORAGE, "filesystem");
        config.set(CheckpointingOptions.CHECKPOINTS_DIRECTORY, "file:///D://flink-data/checkpoints/");

        // Print out S3 credentials for verification
        System.out.println("fs.s3a.access.key: " + config.getString("fs.s3a.access.key", "NOT SET"));
        System.out.println("fs.s3a.secret.key: " + config.getString("fs.s3a.secret.key", "NOT SET"));
        System.out.println("fs.s3a.endpoint: " + config.getString("fs.s3a.endpoint", "NOT SET"));

        // Initialize the file system with the config
        FileSystem.initialize(config, null);

        // Create a streaming execution environment with checkpoints enabled
        StreamExecutionEnvironment env = StreamExecutionEnvironment.getExecutionEnvironment(config);
        env.enableCheckpointing(10000);
        env.getCheckpointConfig().setMinPauseBetweenCheckpoints(5000);
        env.getCheckpointConfig().setCheckpointTimeout(60000);
        env.getCheckpointConfig().setMaxConcurrentCheckpoints(1);
        env.configure(config);

        // Create a TableEnvironment for streaming queries
        StreamTableEnvironment tableEnv = StreamTableEnvironment.create(env);

  • Verified that the Kafka topic (kafka-5s) is producing data continuously.

  • Used local path with file:/// scheme for easier testing before moving to actual S3.
